name: cistern
summary: A stone tank holding the dungeon's water — still surface, a well-head or drain channel, echoes and dripping.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: square or near-square — tanks want even proportions

primary_palette.note: a drawing point — most of the room is water, a single stone curb in the center
columned cistern, roman-style, pillars rising out of the water
decorative cistern — a central fountain feeds the tank
sacred cistern, the water is consecrated or used in rites

story_prompts: a peaceful working cistern feeding the kitchens above
contaminated cistern — something dead is in the water
sacred pool of a forgotten water-cult, still holy, still watched
flooded sub-level where the cistern has overflowed into the hallway
ancient cistern with submerged stairs descending further into the dungeon

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: damp and cold (0.2-0.35), reflections on water

anti_patterns: don't forget the water — cistern without a filled tank is just a room
don't clutter the water surface; reflections and emptiness are the mood
avoid furniture in the water; ladders and pipes only
